ads.title: 1975 Vintage Bally “Bow and Arrow” pinball machine - family owned for 40 years.
ads.description: This is a vintage Bally “Bow and Arrow” pinball machine from the early electromechanical era, and it has been part of my life for nearly four decades. I got this machine when I was 13 years old, and I’m now 50, so this is not something that has been passed around or abused in commercial settings. It has been privately owned and cared for the entire time. It is in perfect working operation. The machine features classic Bally craftsmanship with fully mechanical scoring reels, original artwork, and that authentic vintage pinball feel that newer machines just don’t replicate. The backglass artwork is bright and vibrant, the playfield is clean and well preserved, and the overall presentation is excellent for its age. It powers on, lights up, and has been a great piece both to play and to display. This is a 1 to 4 player machine with original score reels, classic bumpers, bonus features, and all the charm of a true 1960s era pinball. The cabinet shows normal wear consistent with age, but nothing out of the ordinary, and it still presents very well. Machines like this are getting harder to find, especially ones that have been in long term private ownership and not heavily used in arcades or bars. This is a great opportunity for a collector, enthusiast, or anyone looking to own a true piece of vintage arcade history.

ads.title: (Rare, working) 1969 Nutting Industries “Dual I.Q.” Electro-Mechanical Arcade Game
ads.description: (RARE) 1969 Nutting Industries “Dual I.Q.” Electro-Mechanical Arcade Game – NEW IN BOX This is widely considered the world’s first 2-player “computer” trivia game, designed by Nolan Bushnell, who later created Computer Space — the first commercial video arcade game. Bushnell is recognized as one of the founding figures of the arcade industry. Game Details: • Features approximately 7,000 trivia questions displayed via a projected film system • Uses a large internal film reel with integrated answer detection • Players select answers using 5 choices • Incorrect answers trigger a red light and buzzer • Correct answers activate a green light and bell • Beautifully engineered system of relays, sensors, switches, and lights • Iconic “Man on the Moon” marquee illuminates from the top panel Condition: • Brand new in original box (NOS) • Box shows heavy wear from age/storage • Machine was carefully removed and tested one year ago — fully working • Repacked exactly as originally stored • Kept in climate-controlled storage since • Upon request it can be re-opened, re-tested and a video can be taken of the working condition for serious buyers. $3,450 takes it! Feel free to make a fair offer. Located in Raymond Maine Shipping/Pickup: • Can professionally pack and ship anywhere • Shipping available through Woody’s Haul’n Ace or buyer may use their preferred shipper. • Loading dock and forklift available for easy pickup Note: This machine is being listed on behalf of a client. I will connect the buyer directly with the owner to complete payment and arrange pickup or shipping.

ads.author: John Phillips
ads.title: Vintage Arcade Machine – Working – Original 1980 Conversion Cabinet
ads.description: Up for sale is a vintage Ms. Pac-Man arcade machine in good working condition. This is an original early-80s unit, not a modern reproduction. This machine started life as a cabinet and was professionally converted to Ms. Pac-Man using a period-correct conversion kit (common practice in arcades at the time). The game runs on an original Pac-Man board with the Ms. Pac-Man daughterboard installed. Details: Fully playable Ms. Pac-Man Original CRT monitor (picture looks good for its age) Classic joystick + buttons Cabinet is solid with normal wear from age Side art present (shows some wear, see photos) Original wiring and internal components largely intact Includes original-style operator settings card inside Condition: Works and plays Some cosmetic wear consistent with age (scratches, fading, etc.) Not fully restored – great candidate for restoration or to enjoy as-is Notes for collectors: This is a Pac-Man to Ms. Pac-Man conversion, not a factory-dedicated Ms. Pac-Man cabinet. These were widely used in arcades in the early 1980s and are part of authentic arcade history. Pickup only – located in New Hope AL. Bring help, this is heavy. Message me with any questions or if you want additional photos or video of it running.

ads.author: Matthew Haghighi
ads.title: DOUBLE DRAGON Arcade Cabinet – 1987 TAITO – Iconic Beat 'Em Up – Project / CRT / Original Wiring
ads.description: DOUBLE DRAGON Arcade Cabinet – 1987 TAITO – Iconic Beat ’Em Up – Project / CRT / Original Wiring Up for sale is an original Double Dragon arcade cabinet, one of the most important and influential arcade games ever made. Released in 1987 by Taito, Double Dragon is widely credited with defining the side-scrolling beat ’em up genre. This is the game that paved the way for classics like Final Fight, Streets of Rage, and countless others. It was one of the first true co-op brawler experiences, where two players could fight side-by-side through a continuous scrolling world—something that completely changed arcade gaming at the time. If you’re into arcade history, this is a big one. --- CONDITION / DETAILS: This is a project cabinet, and it’s priced accordingly—but it has a lot of the hard stuff already handled. Original CRT monitor – good working display New joysticks installed New buttons installed New control panel overlay New marquee Brand new side art package (cost me ~$200 alone) --- WHAT IT NEEDS: - Game PCB (board) is missing - Cabinet sides need: - sanding + finishing - Bondo work started on damaged corners but needs to be completed - Top panel is rough (see photos) --- WHY THIS IS A GREAT BUY: - Real 1987 Double Dragon cabinet – not a repro - One of the most iconic arcade titles ever made - Already has key upgrades done (controls, artwork) - Includes original CRT, not an LCD swap - Has correct wiring for an easy restoration - Perfect for: - Dedicated Double Dragon restoration - OR a JAMMA / multi-game build --- THE VIBE: This thing has that gritty, authentic late-80s arcade feel—the dragons wrapping the monitor, classic control panel artwork, and that unmistakable cabinet shape. Once finished, this will be a killer piece. --- BOTTOM LINE: This is a solid foundation project with a lot of money already put into parts and artwork. Finish it your way and you’ll have a legit, historically important arcade machine. Priced to sell.
